original_hypothesis.title: ManagedNFTs initialization gap: constructor accepts arbitrary `_managedNFTs` without ownership validation, risking permanent revenue-withdrawal stalls
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The ERC20 constructor assigns `ManagedNFTs = _managedNFTs` without verifying the ERC20 contract owns each NFT’s AccountId token. If an unowned or incompatible address is included, `withdrawFromManagedNFTs()` will revert when calling `withdrawBalancesTo(...)` (requires approved/owner of AccountId). Because withdrawal batching uses a cursor (`nextWithdrawal`), a revert at a specific index can block progress unless operationally handled, potentially preventing revenue aggregation across the portfolio.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete missing validation defect in critical initialization logic. It is a realistic deployment/ops risk that can brick a core maintenance workflow if an invalid address is included or if ownership changes unexpectedly.
deployment_assumptions: At deployment, `_managedNFTs` includes at least one address not owned by the ERC20 contract (or not a LiquidInfrastructureNFT)
Operations expect permissionless/automatic withdrawals to iterate without manual filtering
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LiquidInfrastructureERC20 constructor sets `ManagedNFTs = _managedNFTs;` without checks. contract-deployer.ts deploys with [] but then uses addManagedNFT for ownership-checked adds; no evidence for production deployment arrays.

proof_of_signal.pattern: constructor: ManagedNFTs = _managedNFTs; (no validation) vs addManagedNFT() which validates ownership

preconditions: ManagedNFTs contains at least one contract address where LiquidInfrastructureERC20 is not approved/owner for AccountId
Someone calls withdrawFromManagedNFTs such that iteration reaches that index
The invalid entry is not removed/handled by ops (no removal function exists other than releaseManagedNFT for owned NFTs)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Mismatch between constructor behavior (no checks) and addManagedNFT behavior (checks)
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Deployment configuration determines whether the bug manifests

operational_questions: Are any production deployments initializing with non-empty `_managedNFTs`?
Is there an ops playbook to recover from a bad ManagedNFTs entry (without a remove-by-index function)?
validation_requirements: Attempt to deploy with an unowned NFT in `_managedNFTs` and observe withdrawFromManagedNFTs behavior and cursor progression

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If present, any caller can trigger withdrawals that repeatedly revert at the bad index, preventing revenue aggregation without manual intervention.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Revenue collection halted or requires bespoke off-chain handling; potential delayed distributions
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium to high; may require redeploy/migration or custom ops steps

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: ManagedNFTs contents vs actual ownerOf(AccountId) for each NFT
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Withdrawal calls reverting consistently at the same step/index
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated attempts to withdraw with no progress

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
